findings.problem: Two pivotal revelation beats flatten into synopsis-like taxonomy. Tovin's confession catalogs each sibling in style-bible shorthand ("Riva on a pole," "Daro hearing under-song," and so on), and the closing paragraph again sorts Jun into each sibling's interpretive lane. The effect is mechanically explanatory rather than fully dramatic, which blunts the rawness of the discovery and makes the chapter sound like its own character dossier at the moment it should stay messier and more lived.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:191 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:197, rewrite Tovin's account so she recalls one or two concrete remembered instances from that aftermath instead of sequentially naming every sibling trait; keep her diction rooted in work, damage, and what she physically saw. At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:411, keep the close inside Nami's perception and Jun's ordinary physical reality rather than summarizing how Daro, Riva, Lale, and Iven each categorize him.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised confession at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:191 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:197 no longer runs through all five siblings as a list, and if the close at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:411 expresses Nami's conclusion without a round-robin summary of each sibling's stance. Fail if either passage still reads like rostered family metadata.

findings.problem: The chapter leans on clipped noun/negation fragments as a chapter-wide emphasis device: "Not Jun's name. Not the thought... The fit," "Not me. Not the gate. Not the smoke," "Pattern. Bright line. Fate," and similar stacks. At this density the move stops reading as pressure-earned variation and starts reading as a prose tic. It also violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:187, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:201, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:289, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:307, rebuild the emphasis into complete syntactic units or far fewer, more selective fragments. Keep the close-third urgency and compression, but stop using stacked noun fragments and negation beats as the default intensifier across the chapter.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:187, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:201, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:289, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:307 no longer carry emphasis through repeated stacks such as "Not X. Not Y. The Z." or "Noun. Noun. Noun." Fail if three-or-more clipped narration beats without full sentence structure are still doing the work in those targeted lines.

summary: Structurally, the chapter lands its revelation cleanly: Jun is named, Sereya's culpability sharpens, the family fractures along believable lines, and the mission continues under live pressure. Continuity, canonical past tense, bodily specificity, and most character voices hold. It still fails the review bar because the narration overuses clipped fragment emphasis until it becomes a constitutional prose violation, and two key revelation beats lapse into self-summarizing family taxonomy instead of staying fully dramatic.
